title: Shorthand object properties should be grouped at the beginning or end of an object declaration
description: Shorthand object properties should be grouped at the beginning or end of an object declarationAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellconventiones2015Why is this an issue?Grouping all the shorthand declarations together in an object makes the declaration as a whole more readable. This rule accepts shorthand declarations grouped at either the beginning or end of an object.Noncompliant code examplelet obj1 = { foo, a: 1, color, // Noncompliant b: 2, judyGarland // Noncompliant }Compliant solutionlet obj1 = { foo, color, judyGarland, a: 1, b: 2 }orlet obj1 = { a: 1, b: 2, foo, color, judyGarland }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailab
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
let obj1 = {
  foo,
  a: 1,
  color,  // Noncompliant
  b: 2,
  judyGarland  // Noncompliant
}
compliant:
let obj1 = {
  foo,
  color,
  judyGarland,
  a: 1,
  b: 2
}

checks.message: Shorthand object properties should be grouped at the beginning or end
Shorthand object properties should be grouped at the beginning or end
